Experiment validation reveals that the proposed solution offers Dice Similarity Coefficient of 0.80 for PROMISE12 using 2D U-Net model and 0.81 for NCI-ISBI 2013 using 3D U-Net model. This work can be considered an open and practical benchmark, which can be used by researchers and healthcare practitioners looking for a trusted MRI segmentation pipeline based on conventional deep learning models.

title: FarmScope Agricultural Crop Recommendation With Chatbot using ML and DL
authors: V Janakanandini, B Janani, G Kalaiarasi, M Selvi, R Yogitha, L K Joshila Grace
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: One major part of the Indian economy is agriculture that suffers losses in crop yields due to inappropriate crops-soil interactions. The study has proposed the idea of precision agriculture based on the soil properties, crop history and yield data of crops to give out more reasonable suggestions. It also provides end to end crop control of the decision on crop, water, fertilizer and pest control. Machine learning (RF, kNN) enhances accuracy and training of changing soil. Agribot is an intelligent chatbot based upon neural networks and Natural Language Toolkit (NLTK) that can answer the questions asked by farmers and provide them with real-time advice based on data. The study presents predictive farming with machine learning (RF, kNN) and Agribot chatbot powered by a neural network to integrate soil, crop history and dynamic instructions such that the Indian commercial growers may maximize crop selection, irrigation, fertilizer, and pest management.

title: Advancing Recruitment Equity using AI solutions to combat Motherhood Discrimination in the workplace
authors: Chaitali Wadhwa, Shaharayar Asaf Khan, Anurag Mishra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Motherhood discrimination remains a persistent issue in workplaces worldwide, with women often facing biases, unfair treatment, and limited career opportunities due to their reproductive and caregiving responsibilities. In India, this concern is not only a matter of social equity but also a violation of the fundamental rights enshrined under Articles 14, 15, and 16 of the Constitution, which guarantee equality before the law and prohibit discrimination on grounds of sex. The Maternity Benefit Act, 1961, further safeguards the rights of working mothers. The Supreme Court of India, in the most recent case of Arshnoor Kaur & Anr. vs. UOI, has declared gender-based vacancy allocation as unconstitutional, ordering a merit-based, gender-neutral selection process for all candidates. Despite these legal protections, working mothers continue to face significant biases and barriers in hiring, promotion, and training. This research explores the potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and data-driven systems to detect, analyze, and mitigate gender-based bias in workplace recruitment and career progression. By leveraging machine learning models for resume screening and fairness-aware algorithms, the paper demonstrates how soft computing, in general, and fuzzy logic, in particular, can contribute to more equitable hiring and promotion processes. It investigates the integration of fuzzy logic-based recruitment platforms for transparency and trust in decision-making. By situating technological innovation within legal protections and judicial interpretations, the paper demonstrates how fuzzy logic-based, ethical, and explainable AI systems can advance workplace equity. The significance of this paper lies in bridging conventional anti-discrimination strategies with advanced technological interventions to provide new insights into tackling discriminatory practices against working mothers, which have far-reaching consequences for individuals, organisations, and society as a whole.

title: Quantum Powered Fraud Detection: Identifying Fake URLs, Fake Emails, and Fraud Transactions Using QSVC, QNN, VQC
authors: Roel John, Noel John, Diana Jeba Jingle I, Gnana Prakasi O S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The increasing number of fraudulent transactions and spoofed URLs is a major issue of security in financial systems online. The traditional machine learning models are unable to address the evolving trends of cybercriminals. The present paper will be a comparative analysis of the Quantum Support Vector Classifiers (QSVC), Variational Quantum Classifiers (VQC), and Quantum Neural Networks (QNN) models in detecting fraud in three datasets, malicious URLs, credit card transactions, and phishing emails. We trained the QML models on the individual datasets and tested them based on the accuracy, precision, and the F1-score metrics, and the confusion matrices of the models were obtained. We discovered that, QSVC is highly accurate in classifying benign URLs, but it performs poorly with fraud cases, compared to VQC which is very balanced and has room to optimize. QNN was consistent in learning but needed more complicated circuit architectures to reduce misclassifications. We have found that QML models are promising in the detection of fraud, and more studies are required in the area of ensemble, extended training, and feature engineering. The interactive predictions showed that there were different decision boundaries in the models and hence taking the strengths of both models and combining them.

title: Phishnet: Real-Time URL Analysis for Zero-Day Phishing Detection Using Ensemble Learning
authors: Gaurav Sarraf, Vibhor Pal
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The phishing attack is a matter that is continually threatening the internet users and could result into financial losses, identity theft and other security lapses. The success of the prevention of phishing URLs lies in the automated detection of these types of phishing. The research study demonstrates the new tool to detect phishing URLs and uses some of the Machine Learning (ML) tools. This study presents a powerful ML classification of phishing URLs, and it is based on the Web Page Phishing Detection Dataset. Two ensemble (V1) and baseline (V2) models were generated, which consist of combination of Random Forest (RF), XGBoost, LightGBM and CatBoost and Decision Tree (DT), Naive Bayes (NB) and Logistic Regression (LR) respectively. The performance assessment based on accuracy (Acc), precision (Prec) and recall (Rec), F1-score, AUROC, Cohen Kappa and MCC proves the superiority, with 95.32% accuracy and balanced scores in all metrics of V1, compared to classic classifiers, such as DT, LR, Multinomial Naive Bayes (MNB) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). The strength and generalization ability of the offered model is testified by the ablation experiments on the voting weights and learning rates, as well as the scaling comparisons. The framework provides a functional, efficient, and reliable system of real-time phishing detection, which has a huge practical aspect of cybersecurity defense enhancement and is scalable.

title: Audio-to-Text Conversion with Speaker Identification
authors: Vijayalaxmi H N, Shrimani Jahagirdar, Shwetha K, Radhakrishnan Gopalapillai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The ability to search for specific pieces of information presented by a specific person in audio meetings is a useful capability. The search becomes easy if the transcript of the meeting is annotated with speaker id. This work proposes an effective Audio-to-Text Conversion system with speaker identification. The proposed method uses two parallel pipelines where one pipeline identifies the speaker of each of the audio segments in a multi-speaker meeting session and the other pipeline converts the same audio segment in to corresponding text. The system uses a foundation model for text transcription with timestamping and uses SpeechBrain ECAPA-TDNN encoder for generating speaker embeddings that are 512-dimensional and speaker-robust. The speaker embeddings are classified using a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) classifier. By combining transcription, feature extraction, and classification, it ensures correct segmentation of dialogues and marks them as speaker labels with a confidence threshold for an "Unknown" label. The system classifies known speaker accurately with an F1 score ranging from 82% to 88% in a multi-speaker setup. It significantly enhances the readability and organization in transcribed multi-party conversations.

title: Parallel Temperature Forecasting on a Slurm-Managed Hpc Cluster
authors: Thiri Shwe Sin Htun, Hay Mar Mo Mo Lwin, Win Win Thant
abstract: Data-intensive scientific simulations, such as climate modeling and environmental analysis, deal with large data sets and complex computational methods under the constraints posed by traditional single-node computing systems. High-Performance Computing (HPC) solutions meet these demands by providing distributed execution across multiple compute nodes. However, the performance and scalability of small to medium-sized HPC clusters for such data-intensive forecasting workflows are mainly unknown and undefined. The proposed system uses a scalable cluster with a distributed computing framework aided by shared Network File System (NFS) storage, MUNGE-based authentication, and Simple Linux Utility Resource Management (SLURM) task scheduling for coordinating joint computing and executing multi-node tasks simultaneously and linearly. Parallel computing is achieved through the Message Passing Interface (MPI). It distributes these computations among nodes, and a Euclidean distance similarity approach categorizes historical temperature data to discover similar trends and conditions. The integrated MPISLURM system architecture investigated stable and scalable distributed execution and delivered reliable short-term temperature forecasts through a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 1.39° F. The findings from this research help validate and explain the possible advantages and benefits from such SLURM and scaled-up MPI combinations for fostering higher efficient and repeatable temperature forecasting processes on modest HPC infrastructures.

title: VIBE Project Management: An Autonomous AI-Driven Framework for Intelligent Agile Project Orchestration
authors: Subramanian Palaniappan, Goutham Kacheru
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Traditional agile project management methodologies rely heavily on manual coordination, human intervention, and reactive problem-solving approaches. This paper introduces VIBE (Virtual Intelligence-Based Execution) Project Management, a novel paradigm that leverages artificial intelligence to autonomously orchestrate the complete agile project lifecycle. Drawing inspiration from the emerging concept of "vibe coding" in software development, VIBE Project Management transforms tickets and user stories from static data artifacts into dynamic entities within an intelligent ecosystem. We present Writlix, a production implementation of this framework, which demonstrates autonomous requirement capture through conversational interfaces, intelligent task assignment based on real-time capacity analysis, proactive escalation management, and adaptive monitoring systems. Our approach reduces manual project management overhead by approximately 60-75%, while maintaining adherence to established agile principles including INVEST criteria and Gherkin-format acceptance criteria. This paper details the architectural foundations, intelligent agent subsystems, and practical deployment considerations of VIBE Project Management, positioning it as a transformative approach for organizations seeking to optimize their agile workflows through intelligent automation.

title: A Low-Cost Smart IoT-Enabled Multimodal Sanitation System for Health Monitoring and Urinary–GI Disorders Detection via Bio-Waste Analysis
authors: Purba Pal, Chandril Mallick, Smita Das, Rik Mandal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Many rural and low-income communities lack affordable, continuous health screenings. Poor sanitation and slow diagnosis let infections and chronic diseases spread. This paper describes a low-cost Internet of Things (IoT) sanitation system that analyzes urine and stool in real-time to support health monitoring and early detection of urinary and gastrointestinal problems. The platform uses cheap sensors and open-source micro-controllers. It collects data without direct contact, sends it securely over wireless links, and stores it in the cloud for analysis. Machine learning examines the signals to spot small, early changes and issue alerts. The model achieved an overall system accuracy of 94%, with an average precision of 75%, average recall of 73%, and an F1-score of approximately 74%. The system protects user anonymity and is designed to use little power. It is built to scale, work with other systems, and be easy to use in rural homes and elder-care settings. The goal is to enable prevention, reduce pressure on clinics, and help public-health responses.

title: Efficient Vision Transformers via Token Merging with Head-Wise Attention Correction
authors: Yuki Ichikawa, Masato Motomura, Thiem Van Chu, Daichi Fujiki
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) offer strong performance by modeling global relationships across image patches, but their scalability is limited by the quadratic cost of self-attention. To mitigate this, Token Merging (ToMe) reduces computation by merging similar tokens. This approach relies on proportional attention to preserve the original balance of attention weights after merging. Yet, proportional attention does not fully resolve the attention distortion. It only compensates for a merged token’s influence on other tokens, while ignoring the fundamental distortion of the token’s own self-attention score. This change creates unaddressed distortions that vary across attention heads.In this work, we conduct a detailed analysis of these attention distortions and reveal their dependence on the query–key projection weights of each head. Based on this finding, we propose Head-wise Attention Correction (HAC), a method that adjusts attention scores after token merging by accounting for head-specific characteristics. HAC effectively mitigates the distortions overlooked by proportional attention, maintaining model accuracy while significantly reducing computation. Experiments on ImageNet demonstrate that our method effectively improves the trade-off between efficiency and performance, advancing the development of efficient Vision Transformers via token merging. Code is available at https://github.com/ychikawa/HAC-ToMe

title: Understanding Generative AI Capabilities in Everyday Image Editing Tasks
authors: Brandon Collins, Mohammad Reza Taesiri, Logan Bolton, Viet Dac Lai, Franck Dernoncourt, Trung Bui, Anh Totti Nguyen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) holds significant promise for automating everyday image editing tasks, especially following the recent release of GPT-4o on March 25, 2025. However, what subjects do people most often want edited? What kinds of editing actions do they want to perform (e.g., removing or stylizing the subject)? Do people prefer precise edits with predictable outcomes, or highly creative ones? By understanding the characteristics of real-world requests and the corresponding edits made by freelance photo-editing wizards, can we draw lessons for improving AI-based editors and determine which types of requests can currently be handled successfully by AI editors? In this paper, we present a unique study addressing these questions by analyzing 83k requests with their associated 305k edits from the recent 12 years on the /r/PhotoshopRequest Reddit community. According to human ratings, approximately only 33% of requests can be fulfilled by the best AI editors (including , , ). Interestingly, AI editors perform worse on low-creativity requests that require precise editing than on more open-ended requests. They often struggle to preserve the identity of people and animals, and frequently make non-requested touch-ups. On the other side of the table, VLM judges (e.g., o1) perform differently than human judges and may prefer AI edits over human edits. Code and qualitative examples are available at: https://psrdataset.github.io/.

title: Hybrid Decentralization for Real-Time Messaging: A Smart Contract and IPFS-Based Approach on Ethereum
authors: Venkatesh Babu R, Vignesh D, Sibaath Ahmed S, M P Ramkumar, G S R Emil Selvan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The conventional messaging platform such as WhatsApp or Telegram is based on a centralized server, which fundamentally creates a gateway to censorship, surveillance, and points of failure. In essence, that is damaging to the user privacy and information security. In this paper, therefore, we develop and implement a Decentralized Chat Application (DCA) using Ethereum blockchain. Based on the fundamental capabilities of distributed ledger technologies, namely, immutability, transparency, and trustlessness, we are building a reliable, censorship-resistant chat service. The application operates under Ethereum Smart Contracts to handle decentralized user identities and to store public encryption keys safely as well as establishing chat channels. Our practical message content and media flows are stashed into an effective Peer-to-Peer (P2P) network, potentially stashed in off-chain storage such as Interplanetary File System(IPFS), but all messages are End-to-End Encrypted.The DCA model provides a good framework of the next-gen secure, private, and autonomous social interaction, as it leaves the end-users with complete ownership and control over their digital communication by abandoning the central authority.

title: Enhancing Cryptographic Key Distribution Using Hybrid Quantum Classical Paradigms
authors: S.K. Saravanan, S. Tamilselvan, R. Thalapathi Rajasekaran, A. Sairam, D. Antony Joseph Rajan, A. Kannappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Secure cryptographic key distribution is one of the essential conditions of modern protection of digital communications. The rise of large-scale quantum computing is a very real threat to traditional public-key cryptosystems, with the most commonly used cryptosystems, including RSA and elliptic curve cryptography, being easily broken with the help of quantum algorithms. Despite the demonstrable security offered by Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) through the laws of quantum mechanics, it has a limited range of transmission, expensive hardware, and it is difficult to scale. In order to overcome these shortcomings, this paper will propose a Hybrid Quantum-Classical Key Distribution (HQCKD) framework that combines quantum key generation and classical key management schemes. The proposed solution uses QKD to create a short and very safe quantum key seed, which then scalable classical cryptographic protocols based on long distance and large scale communication are booted up. The frames are tested by means of simulation in real network conditions, the latency, throughput, and security resilience are assessed. It is experimentally proven that the suggested hybrid model attains low end-to-end latency and high scalability with high resistance to both classical and quantum adversaries. The HQCKD framework is much simpler to implement and significantly less expensive in terms of infrastructure and deployment compared to bare QKD systems, which means that it is a realistic and future-proof approach to post-quantum safe communication.

title: Design and Development of High Efficient Multiport Bidirectional DC-DC Converter for Charging Applications
authors: Preethiga. R, Sailesh. S, Sabari Vashan. V, K. P. Suresh, K. Vishnu Murthy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This project presents the design, modeling, and simulation of a MATLAB/Simulink-based on Multiport Bidirectional DC–DC Converter System that enables coordinated energy exchange between two electric vehicles (EVs) and the utility grid. The proposed topology comprises two independent Multiport Bidirectional converter ports, each interfaced to an EV battery, and a common DC-link that connects to the grid through a grid-interface stage. Each port contains dedicated current and voltage control loops and a PWM-based switching stage; a supervisory control unit orchestrates coordinated multiport operation, DC-link voltage regulation, and seamless transitions between Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) modes. The control architecture combines inner-loop current regulators, outer-loop DC-link voltage controllers PWM generation and gating synchronization are implemented in Simulink to produce robust switching signals for the semiconductor devices while avoiding inter-port interference and ensuring proper dead-time and timing alignment. Each EV’s Battery Management System (BMS) is modeled to monitor SOC, voltage, current, and temperature, and to impose protection and charging limits that the supervisory controller respects. A central contribution is the systematic analysis of eight operational modes arising from different ON/OFF combinations of the DC source and the two EV batteries (e.g., source → both EVs, EV1 discharging while EV2 charging, both EVs discharging to grid, etc.). For each mode we analyze power-flow direction, control response, DC-link stability, current sharing, and transition logic. MATLAB/Simulink results demonstrate stable DC-link voltage regulation, coordinated power distribution, effective SOC management.

title: Empirical Evaluation of Software Task Effort Prediction Using Natural Language Descriptions
authors: Surya Sujarwo, Haryono Soeparno, Yulyani Arifin, Ford Lumban Gaol
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This paper offers an empirically grounded analysis of machine learning models that predict effort directly from natural language task input to address the issue of software effort estimation with machine learning. Two different datasets, a proprietary commercial dataset (SiP) and the open-source JOSSE dataset, were used to test combinations of three embedding techniques (TF-IDF+SVD, Word2Vec, and BERT) and four classifiers (Random Forest, LightGBM, Histogram-Based Gradient Boosting, and Feed-Forward Artificial Neural Networks). Based on an improved 7-class effort classification, the assessment assesses 27,729 activities across 100 projects. The results show that the optimal embedding-classifier combination depends on the context. While BERT embeddings with LightGBM exhibit the highest overall performance globally (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F1}=0.355$</tex>, AUC<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{R O C} = 0.787$</tex>), BERT with Random Forest performs better when it comes to per-project deployment (average F1: 0.895 for SiP, 0.817 for JOSSE). All models demonstrated strong ranking ability (AUC-ROC>0.979), confirming their promise in case prioritization even though precise classification is still challenging. This paper also emphasizes how important data quality is, as commercial data produces predictions that are far more reliable. This paper may have an impact on the choice of embedding-classifier combinations for different projects and dataset scenarios.

title: Ai-Driven Gesture and Emotion-Responsive 3D Avatar System for Multilingual Deaf-Mute Communication
authors: Pavithra B, Vishal S, Keerthana S
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: To the deaf and mute people, it is extremely hard to communicate effectively, more so, when in a multilingual and a group of people. In the following paper, a solution to this problem is presented in the form of an AI-driven 3D avatar system turning written or spoken text into expressive sign language animations with inbuilt emotional feedback. The proposed system employs a modular pipeline, which comprises of text/speech preprocessing, natural language processing, glosslevel gesture mapping, emotion recognition and real time 3D avatar generation. It is compatible with a number of languages like English, Tamil and Hindi. Whereas BERT-based classifier considers emotional context to generate similar non-manual facial expressions, transformer-based models are used in text-to-gloss translation. Gestures are made smooth using inverse kinematics, motion synthesis and blending. The system is assessed with the help of standard metrics such as BLEU, rendering latency, F1-score with emotion recognition, BLEU to gauge the translation quality and user satisfaction. The experimental results show that it has an emotion F1-score of 0.89, an average BLEU score of 0.92, and real-time performance with a latency of less than 200 ms. User studies also possess increased expressiveness as well as high usability in comparison with baseline systems. The proposed approach demonstrates the way in which 3D avatars powered by AI may be used to enable the deaf and hard-of-hearing community to communicate in a manner that is inclusive, natural, and emotionally authentic.

title: Neuro-Quantum Cyber Security Defenders for Intelligent Threat Prediction in Healthcare Data Management
authors: G. Harinadha Reddy, G. Murali
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: With the transformation of healthcare systems becoming more common through cloud computing and the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), this rapid digitalization renders these networks more susceptible to advanced cyber risks, especially in a post-quantum world. We propose a Neuro-Quantum Cyber Security Defender (NQ-CSD) approach to proactively protect healthcare data with quantum-resilient data protection while simultaneously preventing intelligent threat prediction based on neuro-inspired circuits. The approach of existing studies mainly primarily focuses on encryption-based techniques, while our model firstly introduces a novel combined behavioral analytics and neuro-quantum (BQ) learning mechanism that verifies the potential malicious entity from accessing data before being able to access it. We experimentally validate the proposed framework against classical neural networks and quantum-inspired models on a healthcare access behavior dataset achieving competitive improvements. In particular, the NQ-CSD model can achieve an accuracy of 95.8 % in addition to very low false positive (3.8 %) and false negative (4.5 %) rates which allows reliable threat detection at high efficiency. This provides a higher level of security in access control, audit trails, and operational continuity for healthcare environments. The outcomes validate that the integration of behavioral intelligence with quantum-inspired security mechanisms offers a scalable, adaptive, and resilient approach for post-quantum healthcare cyber security in future generations.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Differential Privacy on the Reliability of Machine Learning Models: A Case Study on LendingClub Loan Data
authors: Ozgur Ardic, Gunes Aydindogan, Golnoosh Babaei, Paolo Giudici, Talu Karagoz
abstract: Machine learning (ML) models are increasingly used in peer-to-peer (P2P) lending to automate credit scoring and decision-making. However, concerns around model reliability including accuracy, fairness, explainability, robustness and data privacy remain critical barriers to trust these leading models. In this study, we examine how differential privacy (DP), a formal privacy-preserving framework, affects the reliability of ML models in the P2P lending context.

title: Role Artificial Intelligence in Direct Taxation : Opportunities and Futuristic Challenges
authors: Rakesh Kumar, Deepak Kumar, Ayushi Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In contrast to human intelligence, which is based on innate knowledge, artificial intelligence (AI) and big data refer to the mental capacity displayed by robots. AI has had a profound impact on human labour and other stakeholders in the banking and finance industry. The idea of "industry 4.0" has fundamentally changed how businesses function in the modern world. Initial, controlled, defined, established, and digitally orientated are the five maturity levels in the model. There is a growing need to protect, improve, and serve the interests of customers and financial institutions. The financial institution now prioritises technology. The many technology paradigms used in the banking and financial industries are examined in this article. In order to better comprehend and pinpoint possible new areas, the study investigates the function of digital technology in the banking and finance industries. The study investigates the possible technological obstacles that the banking industry may face in the future.

title: Interpreatble Deep Learning for Brain Tumour Analysis: Fusing U-Net Segmentation With Grad-CAM Visual Explanations
authors: Suriya Navaneetha Krishnan K, Sheerin Sitara N, Ravi Prasath S, Rethik Raj R R
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The implementation of Deep Learning (DL) models of critical medical tasks, including brain tumor diagnosis using MRI, is inherently limited by the nature of their black-box decision-making model. In an attempt to close this clinical trust divide, we came up with a new, understandable concept that prioritizes the accuracy of the diagnosis and the evidence that can be tested. The highfidelity U-Net architecture is our methodology, which will be used to conduct semantic segmentation, which is a key action that enables proper delineation of tumor boundaries, size and infiltration. Most importantly, we include Gradientweighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) as the fundamental aspect of Explainable AI (XAI). Grad-CAM offers a visual evidence, which is immediate as it creates a heatmap that is directly overlaid on the MRI. This heatmap accurately demonstrates the exact regions in the tumor that dictated the final classification of the model. The current dual-model system is of great clinical interest: the anatomical accuracy of U-Net and the prediction validity of Grad-CAM, which will confirm that the model is focusing on the correct pathology. Our framework will be the first of its kind to provide a reliable and clinically actionable AI in neuroradiology due to its combination of better localization and visual transparencyor.

title: Identity Verification from Human Scent using Channel Representation of 2D Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Data
authors: Radim Spetlik, Jan Hlavsa, Jana Čechová, Petra Pojmanová, Jiří Matas, Štěpán Urban
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: This study examines the feasibility of employing raw two-dimensional gas chromatography/time-of-flight mass spectrometry (GC×GC–ToF-MS) data for the purpose of human scent identity verification. Unlike techniques that require expert-driven identification of compounds, our framework transforms each GC×GC–ToF-MS sample into a multi-channel image. A comprehensive assessment has been conducted on ten feature-embedding methods, and five spatial-alignment strategies.The evaluation is performed on a newly assembled dataset of 252 individuals, comprising 2,528 raw samples and aggregating around 7.5 TB of data. On our dataset, the best method reaches ≈ 53% true positive rate at a 5% false positive rate without explicit spatial registration. Although this performance is below that of well-established biometrics (e.g., iris verification), the results demonstrate the feasibility of raw-odor verification for scenarios where direct line-of-sight or cooperation may be limited and suggest that, for our acquisition/drift regime, explicit registration is not required to reach the best performance.Code and dataset on github.com.

title: Scalable Smart City Analytics Through SmartFusion-Grid: A Unified Data Engineering Approach
authors: Sai Santhosh Reddy Bhimu
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Urban areas have large amounts of heterogeneous data on different domains like air quality, traffic, and crime, and analyzing this data in real-time is a complex problem. The traditional approach to data analysis is not effective in dealing with large amounts of data, heterogeneous data, and data changing over time because they are not highly scalable, highly adaptable, and focus on a single domain. Keeping this in view, this research proposes a highly scalable data engineering system called SmartFusion-Grid, which is designed to integrate different data sources to achieve dynamic risk forecasting and decision support. The model effectively utilizes data preprocessing, recursive feature selection, and an ensemble-based classification method, where accuracy and computational efficiency trade-offs occur. It includes a real-time risk alert system, where risk conditions are detected and alerts are immediately sent out. The analysis of the proposed model suggests that it is highly effective and outperforms existing models, achieving high accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score of 96.6 %. It was validated that the proposed model can be an effective for improving real-time urban risk monitoring, scalability, and predictive integrity to provide a powerful platform for smart city analytics intelligence and sustainability.

title: Deconstructing Prompt Strategies: Evaluating Generative AI for Latency, Relevance, and Depth in Domain-Specific Tasks
authors: Vishwa Bhatt, Divya Thakar, Jerry Cervantes-Fernandez, Soo Dong Kim, Mira Kim, Jennifer Kim Jin
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: This study investigates how prompt strategies influence the performance of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) models, specifically ChatGPT-4o and DeepSeek. To measure performance, we used four core domains—Business, Healthcare, Neuroscience, and Mathematics—as test areas. A dataset of 1,000 prompts was categorized into five distinct prompt types: Simple, Meta, ReAct, Self-Consistency, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). We evaluated 2,000 AI-generated responses using a structured rubric covering Relevance, Accuracy, Complexity, and Runtime; each scored on a 5-point scale. We trained models to classify prompt types and predict response quality using both the textual content of prompts and human-assigned evaluation scores. Machine learning models, including XGBoost classifiers and Random Forest regressors, were used to estimate response quality and identify relationships among evaluation metrics. Correlation analysis revealed weak to moderate associations between scoring dimensions, while the predictive models showed strong alignment with human evaluations. Findings reveal that ChatGPT-4o excels in general-purpose accuracy and speed, whereas DeepSeek demonstrates greater consistency and complexity in domain-specific tasks. These results emphasize the critical role of prompt design in shaping generative AI behavior. Establishing a rubric-aligned evaluation framework is essential as these tools continue to be deployed across instructional and professional settings. This work supports the development of reliable, human-centered evaluation methods for integrating generative AI into education, tutoring systems, and real-world applications.

title: Style Mate: A Comparative Analysis of Outfit Compatibility Predictor in Fashion Technology Employing Machine Learning Models
authors: Shivaprasad B R, Sudesh Rao, Jayanthi M G, Prasad Krishna Murthy, Murulikrishna H, Prashanth Kannadaguli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The e-commerce industry is quickly evolving because of the AI implementation. The AI intelligence is required to predict an intelligent outfit suggestion to improve the customer experience and it also enhances the customer personalization. The approaches for Machine learning are very effective to dominate the fashion recommendation problems. This study evaluates performances of 4 classical machine learning technique - XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Naïve Bayes - in analyzing the outfit compatibility between clothing items like pants and shirts.. A collected resources was pre-processed and feature engineered with visual indicators such as color histogram, texture and shape embeddings. The models are trained and validated while performances are evaluated through Accuracy, Precision, Recall, ROC- AUC curve. Comparative analysis shows that XGBoost is highly suitable for fashion recommended systems, continuously outperforming Random Forest and KNN in terms of classification accuracy and generalization.

title: The Impact of Product Perceived Quality, Environmental Responsibility, and Brand Trust on Gen Z: Examining the Moderating Effects of Fear of Missing Out and Social Media
authors: Angelica Virginevra Malina More, Robertus Tang Herman
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The Indonesian beauty and personal care market is experiencing significant growth, driven by rising consumer demand and the increasing adoption of digital commerce, particularly in the skincare segment. Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex> has become a key driver of this growth, with their purchasing decisions influenced by factors such as perceived product quality (PPQ), brand trust (BT), and environmental responsibility (ER). Additionally, social media platforms like TikTok and emotional triggers like fear of missing out (FOMO) play a crucial role in shaping their buying behavior. This study explores the impact of these factors on Gen Z's purchasing decisions, with a focus on how social media and FOMO act as moderating variables. By integrating SEM-PLS and decision tree analysis, this research provides valuable insights into the factors driving purchase decisions and offers practical implications for brands targeting this demographic. The findings emphasize the importance of leveraging algorithmic insights and social media analytics to optimize marketing strategies and resonate with Gen Z's values, particularly around sustainability and responsible consumption.

title: Autonomous Multi-Agent Architecture for Intelligent Precision Farming
authors: Ashish B. Manwatkar, Shubhangi J. Kamble, Dnyaneshwar V. Kudande, Pratapbhan Singh Senger, Avinash B. Surnar, Sudnya A. Vanakudre
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The convergence of Industry 4.0 technologies with precision agriculture demands intelligent, adaptive systems capable of autonomous decision-making under energy and latency constraints. This paper presents AMAI-PF (Agentic Multi-Agent Intelligence for Precision Farming), a novel framework that re-imagines cross-layer routing optimization through the lens of autonomous agents and multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL). Unlike traditional centralized approaches, AMAI-PF models each sensor node as an independent intelligent agent with localized decision-making capabilities, enabling emergent collective behavior for energy-efficient, reliable data transmission. The framework integrates role-specialized agents for cluster-head selection, route discovery and network monitoring, coordinated through a decentralized governance mechanism. Our approach combines cross-layer optimization (network, physical, and MAC layers) with Q-learning-based adaptive routing, achieving superior performance metrics. Experimental validation using NS-2 simulations on large-scale precision farming networks (100-500 nodes across 1000×1000m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> farm areas) demonstrates that AMAI-PF reduces average energy consumption by 31.4%, extends network lifetime by 220+ rounds, achieves 97.2% packet delivery ratio and maintains end-to-end latency below 8ms. This represents a significant advancement over state-of-the-art protocols including LEACH, EECRP and FEEC-IIR. The agentic architecture enables autonomous adaptation to dynamic environmental conditions making it particularly suitable for precision agriculture applications in smart farming ecosystems.

title: Advancing Potato Leaf Disease Detection: A YOLOv8n-Based Deep Learning Approach for Mobile and IoT Applications
authors: Debasis Chakraborty, Sandip Haldar, Shemim Begum, Srijani Das
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: This study addresses the urgent need for timely detection of leaf diseases in agriculture to mitigate crop losses and their negative impact on the global economy. We propose a deep learning-based technique for potato leaf disease recognition, utilizing the Ultralytics YOLOv8n model trained on the PlantVillage dataset, with the aim of enabling detection on handheld devices like mobile phones or IoT devices. Evaluation metrics including precision (99.344%), recall (96.955%), F1-score (98.135%), and mAP@50(98.347%) demonstrate the model's robust performance in accurately localizing diseased regions. These results underscore the efficacy of our approach, which has significant potential for precision farming and crop management. Overall, this research contributes to advancing technologies that improve agricultural productivity, resilience, and economic sustainability through efficient disease management.

title: Performance Analysis of Grover’s Algorithm Under Noise: A Simulation-Driven Study with Error Mitigation and Hybrid Bloom–Grover Search
authors: Souvik Ghosh, Amrita Kundu, Samya Muhuri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Classical database join operations are a significant performance bottleneck in data-intensive applications, typically requiring quadratic time proportional to the size of the input. Quantum search algorithms, such as Grover’s algorithm, promise asymptotic speedups for unstructured search and can be adapted to accelerate equality-based joins. In this paper, we present a systematic simulation study of a Grover-based quantum join primitive and a hybrid classical-quantum join scheme, comparing them with a baseline classical nested-loop join. Parameterised Grover circuits have been implemented for marked-item search, together with resource estimation (qubit count, circuit depth, and gate counts), using the Qiskit Aer simulator. The classical join is modelled as a nested-loop equality join over two arrays, while the hybrid scheme uses a classical Bloom filter to reduce the candidate space, followed by a Grover search over the remaining items. To approximate near term hardware, we incorporate depolarizing gate noise and readout noise via Aer noise models and investigate two mitigation techniques: readout error calibration and zero noise extrapolation. Our simulations, conducted in a Python environment without GPU acceleration, demonstrate that the Grover based and hybrid joins exhibit the expected sub-quadratic scaling in runtime compared to the classical nested-loop join for problem sizes up to N = 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">15</sup>. We additionally observe that realistic noise significantly degrades the raw success probability of the quantum join, while combining readout mitigation with zero noise extrapolation partially restores accuracy. These results provide a resource-aware and noise-aware baseline for evaluating quantum-accelerated joins on future fault-tolerant or high-fidelity noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices.

title: A Hybrid Haar Cascade Approach for Novel Drowsiness Detection
authors: Moumita Shee, Aishwaria Roy, Ankana Chakraborty, Rima Bachar, Tanni Saha, Soma Datta
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Road accidents are the leading causes of unnatural deaths worldwide, with distracted driving being one of the most common contributing factors. A primary cause of distracted driving is driver drowsiness. Early detection of drowsiness can help prevent accidents by triggering timely alerts or recommending rest. In this paper, the authors proposed an imagebased drowsiness detection technique that utilize the Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR). To identify facial cues associated with drowsiness, such as yawning, closed eyelids, or partially closed eyes, a novel detection approach has been developed. The method integrates EAR thresholding with a modified Haar Cascade model. This technique aims to enhance driver alertness and reduce the likelihood of fatigue-related accidents. The proposed method achieved 92.21 percent of accuracy which is better than the existing methods.

title: Sentiment Analysis of Public Feedback on Government Schemes using Social Media
authors: Yesaswini Arikatla, T. Lakshmi Surekha, Hemalatha Jonnala, Swetha Talluri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Public sentiment is an essential part of many governments in analyzing their welfare programs effectiveness. This study introduces a transformer-based system that detects public sentiment through social media feedback about the various government schemes, like the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) program data that researchers collected from YouTube and Twitter. The system divides every comment and post into three distinct sentiment categories which include positive, neutral, and negative categories. To address dataset imbalance, a data balancing method is used to correct the dataset imbalance problem because it helps to present minority groups more accurately while achieving unbiased results. The system uses a BERT-based transformer model which performs multi-class sentiment analysis through text normalization and subword tokenization methods. Stratified sampling methods is used to conduct both model training and evaluation activities. The experimental results show that class imbalance treatment leads to better macro F1-score results which enable more equitable performance ratings across different sentiment groups. The trained model operates through a Flask-based API which connects to a web dashboard that provides instant sentiment assessment and trend observation and data presentation. The proposed system shows how contextual transformer architectures work for monitoring public sentiment at large scale and backing data-based governance operations.

title: Federated Learning for Alzheimer's Disease: Integrating Neuroimaging and Genomic Biomarkers Using Multi-Center MRI Data
authors: Ritu Aggarwal, Bhavana, Neha Dutta, Eshaan Aggarwal
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Alzheimer's Disease is the leading disease cause found in adults. When machine learning is applied for different disease detection and prediction such as Alzheimer's and diabetes, this disease affects people in the populations found around the world. Every year there will increase the disease rate as it is alarming. Neurodegenerative changes remain perceived and brain effected outstanding to Alzheimer's disease. In healthcare, it affects the family and human health, and each individual relates to social, economic the other financial fronts. In the early stage, it is difficult to predict the disease. Every year the incident rate s is very alarming which has an increasing rate.at an early stage of AD is more effective and difficult to predict as compared to a later stage. Various techniques of Federated learning are employed for the prediction of disease which are measured with the parameters used in machine learning. The proposed methodology can be used by physicians to diagnose illness estimate. It calculates the several phases of illness in persistent such as dementia, very low dementia, low dementia, and average dementia. The proposed method achieves accuracy 98.12 for a 2.1 -segment duration. The results were obtained for real-time analysis and classification the Alzheimer's disease.

title: MultiModal AI for Advanced Spam Detection: Securing Enterprise Communication Channels
authors: Gaurav Sarraf, Adarsh Reddy Bilipelli
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Spam messages have been a major problem in online communication. Privacy is invaded, financial fraud is enabled, and unwarranted spamming occurs in one's inbox. Most spam filtering programs continue to utilize obsolete machine learning techniques. The paper presents a transformer-based model and gradient boosting to provide a holistic solution to the phishing email detection problem. Gradient boosting classifiers LightGBM (Light Gradient Boosting Machine), XGBoost (Extreme Gradient Boosting), and CatBoost (Categorical Boosting) demonstrate strong predictive capability, with CatBoost achieving the highest F1-score. Optuna tuning also increased the accuracy of the model, tuned LightGBM achieved 96.40 test accuracy and 99.45 ROC-AUC. Stacking techniques such as ensemble methods have been used to boost generalization with a test F1-score of 95.74%. The effectiveness of the proposed method is justified by the comparative analysis with such classic models as SVM and RNN, and the stability of transformer designs is justified by the ablation studies. The proposed approach is proven to be superior over traditional models such as SVM (Support Vector Machine), NB (NaiveBayes) and RNN (Naive Bayes) through comparison analysis, and the ablation studies confirm the stability of the transformer configurations. The findings suggest that a combination of boosting and transformer models may be considered to filter the enterprise-level spam, and it is a powerful solution, flexible to emerging threats.

title: A Legally Trustworthy and Sustainable AI Framework for Automated Legal Systems
authors: Aarthy Jonathan Kennedy, Arun D Raj, A. Sivanandam, Krishna Kishore Padala, Chesha P. Chellappan, Sreeja B G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The growing integration of artificial intelligence into the court systems has become a matter of serious concerns in terms of law, morality, and ecology. Current AI methods of automated legal uses are more interested in predictive quality and less concerned with regulation compliance, justice, and environmental influence. In this paper, the authors come up with a compliance-conscious machine intelligence system that would facilitate legally reliable and ecologically sound AI in automated legal systems. The framework directly incorporates the legal limitations, ethical governance structures, and environmental impact measures in the machine learning training and evaluation process. The model is proposed as a multi-dimensional evaluation program, which considers models in predictive performance, compliance with regulations, fairness, explainability, and energy efficiency. The experimental findings have shown that the offered approach greatly enhances the levels of legal compliance and ethical alignment but preserves the highly competitive predictive performance and minimizes the levels of energy consumption and carbon emissions, in comparison with traditional models. These results indicate that it is possible to implement AI systems, not just effective but legally justifiable, ethically accountable, and environmentally friendly in actual legal contexts.

title: Deep Learning–Driven Intelligent System for Early and Accurate Skin Cancer Diagnosis
authors: Monelli Ayyavaraiah, Dasari Sreenath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Skin cancer, particularly melanoma, poses a significant global health challenge, and early diagnosis remains crucial for improving survival rates. Traditional dermatoscopic examination, though effective, is prone to inter-observer variability and limited scalability. In this work, we propose a deep learning–driven intelligent system for early and accurate skin cancer diagnosis that integrates image-based and patient-level information. The framework employs a U-Net++ segmentation module to isolate lesion regions, followed by a hybrid CNN– Transformer backbone to jointly model local texture features and global contextual dependencies. To further enhance diagnostic precision, metadata fusion incorporates clinical attributes such as age, gender, and UV exposure. The classification layer leverages a combined weighted cross-entropy and focal loss function to address class imbalance, while Grad-CAM and SHAP explainability modules provide transparent and clinically interpretable predictions. Experiments conducted on the ISIC and HAM10000 datasets demonstrate that the proposed system achieves 95% accuracy, 0.94 F1-score, and 0.98 AUC, outperforming widely used baselines such as ResNet50, DenseNet121, and EfficientNet. The results highlight the robustness and clinical relevance of the framework, making it a promising tool for real-world dermatological applications.

title: White Blood Cell Recognition Through Transfer Learning and Deep Fine-Tuning
authors: P. Yuvarani, Divakar A, Dinesh Kumar S, Dinesh Karthik Rajan D
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Proper identification of white blood cells (WBCs) is an important procedure in the diagnosis and management of the hematological disorders. Manual procedures in the identification of WBC are time consuming, subjective, and prone to error. Automated deep learning-based multiclass WBC classification (i.e. classifying cells to EOSINOPHIL, LYMPHOCYTE, MONO-CYTE, and NEUTROPHIL). The two state-of-the-art transfer learning models GoogLeNet and EfficientNetB0 were used and trained on publicly available Blood Cell Images dataset on Kaggle that consists of a total of 12,517 images in training, validation, and test split. The preprocessing of data, such as image resizing, normalization, and augmentation, were implemented on a large scale to improve model generalization and overcome the problem of the imbalance of the classes. The proposed framework was highly accurate, had strong classification, and GoogLeNet gave a test accuracy of 98.78% on average, slightly outperforming EfficientNetB0. Individual analyses based on precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrices, and ROC curves reveal the suitability of the models in dissimilarity of the visual appearance of WBCs. More so, comparative analysis helps to identify the trade-offs of model accuracy and computational efficiency that could be used in practice by the deployment of the model in clinical and laboratory environments. The article demonstrates the possibilities of transfer learning and fine-tuned deep learning models to automatize the process of WBC classification, minimize human error, and deliver prompt and dependable diagnostic support.

title: RAT4D: Rig and Animate Objects without Surface Templates in 4D
authors: Mosam Dabhi, Simon Lucey, László A. Jeni
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present a surface-template-free method for reconstructing dense, rigged (re-animatable) 3D models from monocular videos. By combining robust pose optimization with differentiable Gaussian splatting, this work bridges the gap between flexible template-free approaches and the visual quality of template-based methods. Starting from noisy 2D keypoints, we refine 3D poses through kinematic and temporal constraints, then attach Gaussian primitives to the optimized skeleton for differentiable supervision and rendering. We demonstrate dense, rigged (re-animatable) 3D models without surface templates across humans, animals, insects, and everyday articulated objects, and we empirically show that closing the rendering–pose loop improves 3D lifting from noisy landmarks. A key enabler of these template-free reconstructions is our kinematic optimization, which reduces 3D pose error by 20–25% relative to template-free baselines; at the same time, our results approach template-based visual metrics (PSNR/SSIM within 5%). We also demonstrate the method’s practical utility by detecting and correcting geometric inconsistencies in AI-generated videos. While limited to articulated subjects with detectable keypoints, the approach provides a practical pipeline that serves as a drop-in refinement to improve 3D lifting in existing pipelines and enables the creation of rigged 3D assets from casual captures when expensive surface templates (e.g., MoCap-derived) are unavailable.

title: Crowd Behavior Monitoring for Panic or Stampede Prediction using Video Analytics and Multi-Parameter Sensor Fusion
authors: Satvika S S, Tharik Grishan S, Loveline Zeema J
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Stampede incidents at mass gatherings represent a critical public safety challenge, demanding proactive intervention systems. Traditional crowd monitoring employs reactive detection mechanisms that provide insufficient time for effective emergency response. This research presents a comprehensive Multi-Stage Crowd Stampede Prediction and Risk Fusion System that integrates four critical, heterogeneous risk parameters—crowd density, abnormal movements, crowd pressure, and physiological temperature through a weighted fusion model to significantly extend prediction lead time. Our system implements a real-time video analytics pipeline using sliding window analysis (10-frame sequences). Feature extraction is handled by specialized deep learning models: CSRNet for density estimation, a Spatiotemporal Autoencoder for abnormal movement detection, and Random Forest regressors for predicting latent pressure and temperature proxies. The four normalized risk indicators are fused using a weighted linear combination (highest weight: 0.40 for abnormal movements) to generate a unified risk score, enabling a three-tier classification: SAFE (score < 0.30), ALERT (0.30–0.60), and DANGER (score ≥ 0.60). Validation on various datasets shows that the Random Forest models achieve high predictive accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.98), and that anomaly detection (F1-score: 88.6%) and accurate transition through risk states are successful. The system offers a stable and open framework while processing video frames at real-time speeds. This framework allows proactive crowd management interventions that may help prevent stampede casualties during major public events by extending prediction horizons and integrating multiple risk modalities.

title: Coconut Water Based Electrolyte Gel
authors: M Sharmila, V Ranjani, M Sanjay, S Sudha, V Sivasankari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Acquisition electrodes are a vital element in the acquisition of physiological signals (e.g., ECG, EEG, EMG). The market for disposable electrodes relies on electrolytic gels to optimally acquire those signals; and while gel electrodes have provided compatibility, and price, they have resulted in irritation to the skin and significant environmental consequences. This paper represents a biocompatible, environmentally friendly, and inexpensive alternative. Coconut water contains naturallly occurring electrolyte such as potassium, sodium, and magnesium which also enhances conduction, and agar gum is used as a gelling agent with stability at the time of application. The development of the coconut water electrolyte gel involved the following main process: Filtration and sterilization of the coconut water; addition of electrolytes; preparation of the gel; and preservation with a natural anti-void (neem extract). Conductivity testing and a broad range of biomedical application supported its use as compared with market (commercial hydrogels). The disposable electrodes are readily biocompatible, cost effective, and biodegradable, but we would need to contend with hydratation preservation and shelf life. Future scope will include the incorporation of carbon-based conductive materials for improved signal qualities, and modifying the formulation development to ensure shelf stability, making it a valuable contribution to sustainable biomedicine engineering.

title: LASER: Lip Landmark Assisted Speaker Detection for Robustness
authors: Le Thien Phuc Nguyen, Zhuoran Yu, Yong Jae Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Active Speaker Detection (ASD) aims to identify who is speaking in complex visual scenes. While humans naturally rely on lip-audio synchronization, existing ASD models often misclassify non-speaking instances when lip movements and audio are unsynchronized. To address this, we propose Lip landmark Assisted Speaker dEtection for Robustness (LASER), which explicitly incorporates lip landmarks during training to guide the model’s attention to speech-relevant regions. Given a face track, LASER extracts visual features and encodes 2D lip landmarks into dense maps. To handle failure cases such as low resolution or occlusion, we introduce an auxiliary consistency loss that aligns lip-aware and face-only predictions, removing the need for landmark detectors at test time. LASER outperforms state-of-the-art models across both in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks. To further evaluate robustness in realistic conditions, we introduce LASER-bench, a curated dataset of modern video clips with varying levels of background noise. On the high-noise subset, LASER improves mAP by 3.3 and 4.3 points over LoCoNet and TalkNet, respectively, demonstrating strong resilience to real-world acoustic challenges.

title: Diverse Sketch Colorization with Content-Enhanced Style Representation and Recolorization Distillation
authors: Shuangming Mao, Haixiang Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Sketch colorization is highly demanded in the field of art, as it offers a valuable tool for artists, designers, and illustrators to explore novel possibilities and express their creativity. Given a sketch, it can be colorized in various styles, such as rendering a facial sketch with different hair colors. However, due to the inherent scarcity of information in sketches, the resulting colorized images often exhibit noticeable artifacts and lack of diversity. In this paper, we aim to generate informative-style representations that can effectively compensate for missing content information in sketches while enabling diverse generations. We improve style granularity by extracting style information from CLIP, and achieve the disentanglement of content and style by establishing semantic correspondence between sketches and color images in the CLIP space. To further improve the semantic accuracy, we simultaneously train a recolorization model end-to-end. The recolorization model shares the same style space as the colorization model. In this way, we can construct multiple pseudo-sketch-image pairs for each sketch, which are used to provide pixel-level supervision for the colorization model, thus significantly facilitating the learning of semantic correspondence. Experiments demonstrate that our method effectively mitigates artifacts in colorized results and produces more semantically rich colors.

title: Better Safe Than Sorry? Overreaction Problem of Vision Language Models in Visual Emergency Recognition
authors: Dasol Choi, Seunghyun Lee, Youngsook Song
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have shown capabilities in interpreting visual content, but their reliability in safety-critical scenarios remains insufficiently explored. We introduce VERI, a diagnostic benchmark comprising 200 synthetic images (100 contrastive pairs) and additional 50 real-world images (25 pairs) for validation. Each emergency scene is paired with a visually similar but safe counterpart through human verification. Using a two-stage evaluation protocol (risk identification and emergency response), we assess 17 VLMs across medical emergencies, accidents, and natural disasters. Our analysis reveals an "overreaction problem": models achieve high recall (70–100%) but suffer from low precision, misclassifying 31–96% of safe situations as dangerous. Seven safe scenarios were universally misclassified by all models. This "better-safe-than-sorry" bias stems from contextual over-interpretation (88–98% of errors). Both synthetic and real-world datasets confirm these systematic patterns, challenging VLM reliability in safety-critical applications. Addressing this requires enhanced contextual reasoning in ambiguous visual situations.

title: Style-Friendly SNR Sampler for Style-Driven Generation
authors: Jooyoung Choi, Chaehun Shin, Yeongtak Oh, Heeseung Kim, Jungbeom Lee, Sungroh Yoon
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent text-to-image diffusion models generate high-quality images but struggle to learn new styles, which limits the personalized content creation. In response, style-driven generation has become a popular task, wherein users supply reference images capturing the target style, complemented by text prompts that specify stylistic cues. Fine-tuning is a common approach, yet it often blindly utilizes pre-training configurations without modification, especially for noise schedules defined in terms of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), which determines the amount of image information available at each denoising step. We discover that stylistic features predominantly emerge at low SNR range, leading current fine-tuning methods using regular noise schedules to exhibit suboptimal style alignment. We propose the Style-friendly SNR sampler, which focuses the fine-tuning on low SNR range where stylistic features emerge. We demonstrate improved generation of novel styles that cannot be described solely with a text prompt, enabling high-fidelity personalized content creation.

title: Test-Time Consistency in Vision Language Models
authors: Shih-Han Chou, Shivam Chandhok, James J. Little, Leonid Sigal
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved impressive performance across a wide range of multimodal tasks, yet they often exhibit inconsistent behavior when faced with semantically equivalent inputs—undermining their reliability and robustness. Recent benchmarks, such as MM-R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, highlight that even state-of-the-art VLMs can produce divergent response across semantically equivalent inputs, despite maintaining high average accuracy. Prior work addresses this issue by modifying model architectures or conducting large-scale fine-tuning on curated datasets. In contrast, we propose a simple and effective test-time consistency framework that enhances semantic consistency without supervised re-training. Our method is entirely post-hoc, model-agnostic, and applicable to any VLM with access to its weights. Given a single test point, we enforce consistent predictions via two complementary objectives: (i) a Cross-Entropy Agreement Loss that aligns predictive distributions across semantically equivalent inputs, and (ii) a Pseudo-Label Consistency Loss that draws outputs toward a self-averaged consensus. Our method is plug-and-play, and leverages information from a single test-input itself to improve consistency. Experiments on the MM-R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> benchmark show that our framework yields substantial gains in consistency across state-of-the-art models, establishing a new direction for inference-time adaptation in VLMs.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: Sketch2Stitch: GANs for Abstract Sketch-Based Dress Synthesis
authors: Faizan Farooq Khan, Eslam Abdelrahman Bakr, Davide Morelli, Marcella Cornia, Rita Cucchiara, Mohamed Elhoseiny
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In the realm of creative expression, not everyone possesses the gift of effortlessly translating their imaginative visions into flawless sketches. More often than not, the outcome resembles an abstract, perhaps even slightly distorted representation. The art of producing impeccable sketches is not only challenging but also a time-consuming process. Our work is the first of this kind in transforming abstract, sometimes deformed garment sketches into photorealistic catalog images, to empower the everyday individual to become their own fashion designer. We create Sketch2Stitch, a dataset featuring over 65,000 abstract sketch images generated from garments of Dress Code [40] and VITON HD [6], two benchmark datasets in the virtual try-on task. Sketch2Stitch is the first dataset in the literature to provide abstract sketches in the fashion domain. We propose a StyleGAN-based generative framework that bridges freehand sketching with photorealistic garment synthesis. We demonstrate that our framework allows users to sketch rough outlines and optionally provide color hints, producing realistic designs in seconds. Experimental results demonstrate, both quantitatively and qualitatively, that the proposed framework achieves superior performance against various baselines and existing methods on both subsets of our dataset. Our work highlights a pathway toward AI-assisted fashion design tools, democratizing garment ideation for students, independent designers, and casual creators.

title: BankDefender - A Cognitive Dark Web Threat Intelligence Framework for Banking Sector
authors: Dineshkumar K, Suresh M, Russia S, Merlyn A S, Nandhini S, Sivadharsini S
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Background: The banking sector has experienced rapid digitalization alongside a dramatic increase in cyber threats from the dark web where approximately 70% of financial breaches are first found on underground forums before being identified in traditional information security monitoring tools, all of which are calling for actionable, operational, and sector specific cyber threat intelligence. Methods: BankDefender leverages transformer models (DistilBERT for classification; and BERT-NER for indicator of compromise extraction) to process and automatically analyze dark web data, enrich the indicators of compromise with VirusTotal and AlienVault OTX APIs, construct Neo4j knowledge graphs that connect threat actors to banking assets, utilizing 42,000 relationships, and implements multi-factor risk scoring, making use of banking specific criticality weights. Results: BankDefender achieved classification accuracy of 94.3% and indicator of compromise extraction precision of 90.8% while processing 2,800 records per minute with 1.6 seconds latency, classifying 62% Medium, 28% High, and 10% Low risk threats, while reducing false positives by 15 %. Conclusion: The integration of version 2.1 of the STIX Framework for banking-sector Cybersecurity with BankDefender gives evidence to AIbased, domain-specific Threat Intelligence and Explainable Risk Prioritization capabilities of BankDefender as a Cybersecurity Solution.

title: Research on Grid Fault Detection Technology Based on BeiDou Technology
authors: Zhenwu Xie, Xiaoqian Liu, Yulei Jia, Yi Zhou, Xinlei Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Renewable Energy and Electrical Technology (ICREET)
abstract: This paper proposes a power grid fault detection method based on Beidou space-time synchronization technology. Combining high-precision timing with synchronized phasor measurement, this method can accurately locate and quickly identify distribution line faults. The system utilizes a dynamic time warping algorithm to extract transient waveform features, significantly improving the accuracy of fault diagnosis. The method is suitable for multi-point coordinated monitoring of medium-voltage distribution systems.

title: Analysis of Voltage Fluctuations in Photovoltaic Power Stations Considering Meteorological Factor Weights on Similar Days
authors: Kexin Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: The dominant degree of meteorological factors affecting the output of photovoltaic stations varies under different weather conditions. Directly analyzing the output voltage using meteorological factors cannot accurately feedback its fluctuations. Therefore, a study was conducted on the analysis of voltage fluctuations in photovoltaic stations considering days with similar weights of meteorological factors. After calculating the maximal information coefficient (MIC) coefficient of the photovoltaic power generation impact factor, use it as a weight parameter. Select the TOP-N with the highest similarity to the analyzed day based on the weight of meteorological factors as the similar day for voltage waveform analysis. Calculate the distribution probability of random variables composed of meteorological factors on similar days, fit the power generation of photovoltaic stations within a time window, and output the voltage time series under rated current by sliding the time window. In the test results, the analysis of the output voltage waveform of the photovoltaic power station has achieved a high degree of fitting with the actual implementation, with an R2 of 0.90 or above, which can accurately feedback the fluctuation state of the voltage.

title: Improved Flood Management and Risk Communication Through LLM
authors: Imaya Ranaweera, Susara S Thenuwara, H Waruna H Premachandra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal flood decision-support system that combines Large Language Models (LLMs), a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline, and a Flood Knowledge Graph (FKG) for operational flood risk communication. The system ingests GIS layers, real-time hydrological sensor streams, meteorological forecasts, and social media posts into a Neo4j-based FKG, which is then queried by a RAG framework to ground LLM outputs in current and historically validated flood conditions. A human in the loop governance layer validates critical messages before dissemination, ensuring that generated alerts, summaries, and evacuation suggestions remain traceable and policy compliant. The hydrological forecasting component achieved a Nash Sutcliffe Efficiency (NSE) of 0.89, while knowledge graph grounding reduced factual hallucinations in LLM responses by 78.6% compared to a text-only baseline. The integrated system further supported dynamic risk classification and real-time visualization of optimized evacuation routes. These results demonstrate that the proposed architecture can transform raw, heterogeneous flood data into reliable, actionable intelligence for emergency managers, moving beyond generic chat responses toward deployable, decision-grade flood early warning and response.

title: ICONIC-444: A 3.1-Million-Image Dataset for OOD Detection Research
authors: Gerhard Krumpl, Henning Avenhaus, Horst Possegger
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Current progress in out-of-distribution (OOD) detection is limited by the lack of large, high-quality datasets with clearly defined OOD categories across varying difficulty levels (near- to far-OOD) that support both fine- and coarse-grained computer vision tasks. To address this limitation, we introduce ICONIC-444 (Image Classification and OOD Detection with Numerous Intricate Complexities), a specialized large-scale industrial image dataset containing over 3.1 million RGB images spanning 444 classes tailored for OOD detection research. Captured with a prototype industrial sorting machine, ICONIC-444 closely mimics real-world tasks. It complements existing datasets by offering structured, diverse data suited for rigorous OOD evaluation across a spectrum of task complexities. We define four reference tasks within ICONIC-444 to benchmark and advance OOD detection research and provide baseline results for 22 state-of-the-art post-hoc OOD detection methods.

title: Chiplet-Based Hardware Security and IC Traceability: A Review of PUFs, Die Identifiers, and Secure Packaging Techniques
authors: Pradeep Chhawchharia, Hiranya Soni, Parth Kalyana, Jay Patel, Mahesh Dangi, Nitin Purohit
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The transition from monolithic System-on-Chip (SoC) architectures to disaggregated chiplet-based System-in-Package (SiP) designs offer significant advantages in yield, scalability, and heterogeneous integration. However, this paradigm shift introduces a fragmented supply chain and physically accessible die-to-die (D2D) interconnects, fundamentally altering the hardware security landscape. This review paper provides a comprehensive analysis of security primitives and traceability frameworks tailored for 2.5D and 3D integrated circuits. We critically examine Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs) for hardware root-of-trust, distinguishing between weak and strong architectures suitable for chiplet authentication. We evaluate advanced IC traceability techniques, contrasting electronic chip IDs (ECIDs) with physical fingerprinting. Furthermore, we survey secure packaging innovations, including active interposer monitoring and shielding, alongside a detailed discussion on emerging industry standards such as UCIe 2.0, OCP Caliptra, and NIST supply chain guidance. Finally, we identify open challenges regarding reliability under thermal stress and the necessity for unified security interoperability standards.

title: DTMIR-Pro: Domain Translation with Prompt-based Latent-Space Generalization for Multi-Weather Image Restoration
authors: Ashutosh Kulkarni, Prashant W. Patil, Santosh Kumar Vipparthi, Subrahmanyam Murala, Balasubramanian Raman
